summary: Matched more of the sysdolphin baselib memory-card async callback hsd_803A949C. The diff re-modeled command-buffer access through CMD_* offset macros over hsd_804D1138, reused the shared CardState layout with file_info and digest fields, marked the command index hsd_804D7980 volatile, and updated the callback prototype to take chan plus arg1. decomp.dev reported hsd_803A949C improving from 58.78% to 93.40% (+1679 bytes), though the PR title says 92%.

key_files.role: Main hsd_803A949C matching rewrite: command-buffer macros, CardState usage, volatile index, callback logic reshaping.
Updated hsd_803A949C prototype from one argument to CARD-style two-argument callback signature.
Not directly changed, but its existing CardState definition became the reused state type instead of a local CardStateExt duplicate.

what_changed: Changed extern s32 hsd_804D7980 to extern volatile s32 hsd_804D7980.
Removed local CardStateExt and CardCmdEntry definitions and removed hsd_803A949C_GetEntry.
Added CardBufEntry and macros CMD_S32(off), CMD_STATE, CMD_PTR(off), and CMD_TYPE to access command data from hsd_804D1138 plus offsets indexed by hsd_804D7980.
Changed hsd_803A949C_Close and hsd_803A949C_IconSize to take CardState* and use shared fields such as file_info and digest.
Changed hsd_803A949C signature from void hsd_803A949C(s32 arg1) to void hsd_803A949C(s32 chan, s32 arg1), mirrored in the header.
Replaced many entry->field accesses with CMD_S32/CMD_PTR offset accesses, including command type, state pointer, slot, file id, sequence/version-like values, data pointers, and lengths.
Restructured several switch cases to put block parsing and validation inside each branch rather than after a shared block, including file-id, x12, and memcmp checks.
For digest validation/copy cases, replaced state->x430 accesses with CMD_STATE->digest and used state->x8 directly rather than a sector_size local.
In the create/open-related case, added a loop that waits up to 10 iterations for state->file_info.fileNo to become non-negative before copying it to state->x20.
In case 13, replaced CardBlock pointer reuse with direct byte indexing from state->x0 plus offsets for slot 0 and nonzero slots, then calls hsd_803AC558 with the payload pointer.
At function end, clears CMD_TYPE instead of entry->x0, resets hsd_804D799C, and advances hsd_804D7980 modulo 128.
smart_moves: Using volatile on hsd_804D7980 is a strong matching tactic for async callback state: it prevents the compiler from assuming the command index is stable across accesses.
The CMD_S32(off) macro models the command buffer as overlapping offset windows over hsd_804D1138 rather than forcing a misleading fixed struct; this also lets CMD_TYPE be assigned as an lvalue.
Reusing CardState avoids a local duplicate CardStateExt and lets known fields like file_info and digest drive clearer and more consistent codegen.
Adding the unused chan parameter aligns hsd_803A949C with Dolphin CARD async callback shape, which matters even when call sites cast to void (*)(s32, s32).
Keeping some validation duplicated inside branches, rather than factoring it into a single shared block, preserves the control-flow shape needed for matching.
Switching some CardBlock reads to direct byte-indexed loads documents that exact load shape can matter more than a tidy struct access in stubborn matching work.
decomp_lessons: For async CARD callbacks, prefer the real callback ABI signature with channel/result-style parameters even when one parameter is unused.
Before inventing local extension structs, search nearby baselib headers for shared state structs; CardState in hsd_3AA7.h already had the needed file_info and digest fields.
A command queue may not decompile cleanly as one C struct. Offset macros over a byte buffer can be a practical interim representation when the assembly accesses differently shifted views.
Globals touched by callback or scheduler state may need volatile to reproduce repeated loads and stores.
Matching long switch callbacks often requires preserving branch-local ordering of close calls, CRC/hash checks, memcpy/memcmp, and error assignment instead of refactoring for readability.
When a helper struct creates the wrong code shape, direct byte addressing from the backing buffer can be a valid matching tactic, as seen in the case 13 CardBlock handling.
Header prototypes are part of matching work: correcting hsd_803A949C in hsd_3A94.h kept declarations consistent with the implementation and CARD callback usage.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based symbol names for unknown/static helpers: hsd_803A949C_Close, hsd_803A949C_IconSize, and hsd_803A949C_FileId.
Used CardBufEntry for a temporary command-buffer element type while retaining x0/x4/x8-style unknown field names.
Used all-caps macros for command-buffer views: CMD_S32, CMD_STATE, CMD_PTR, and CMD_TYPE.
Moved from local xC/x430 field names to shared CardState field names file_info and digest.
Named the new callback parameter chan while leaving the second parameter as arg1, reflecting known channel semantics but weak evidence for the second parameter's final name.
Kept CardBlock fields as offset names x10, x11, x12, and x20 where semantic names were not established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Mark hsd_804D7980 volatile to force reloads of the current command index.
Use CMD_S32(off) as (((CardBufEntry*) (hsd_804D1138 + off))[hsd_804D7980].x0) to match indexed loads from shifted command-buffer offsets.
Make CMD_TYPE an lvalue macro so the final command clear compiles as a store through the same buffer view.
Add an unused leading callback parameter chan to match the two-register CARD callback ABI.
Remove the local sector_size variable and use state->x8 directly in digest/data comparisons, likely influencing register allocation.
Move block pointer creation until after hsd_803B31CC and hsd_803A949C_Close checks, and duplicate comparisons inside both branches.
Use direct state->x0 byte loads for case 13 file id and version fields instead of hsd_803A949C_FileId(block).
Add a bounded 10-iteration fileNo polling loop before assigning state->x20 from state->file_info.fileNo.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review approvals were captured in the slice, and the PR body was empty.
decomp-dev[bot] reported one unmatched-item improvement for GALE01: main/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94 hsd_803A949C improved by +1679 bytes, from 58.78% to 93.40%.
follow_up_queries: Search hsd_3AA7.c and related baselib code for command-buffer definitions that could replace CMD_S32/CMD_PTR macros with a better typed command struct later.
Investigate semantics for CMD_S32 offsets 0x18, 0x1c, 0x20, 0x24, 0x28, and 0x30 across command types.
Confirm final naming for hsd_803A949C's second callback parameter; evidence supports chan for the first parameter but leaves arg1 semantic naming weak.
Look for similar async callback functions that still have one-argument prototypes or non-volatile command queue indices.
After more of hsd_3A94/hsd_3AA7 is matched, revisit whether duplicated case 1 and case 16 logic can be named or structurally explained.
